signal_id.description: Universal signal identifier referencing the data provider's catalog. Use this to verify authorization and look up signal definitions.

signal_agent_segment_id.description: Opaque activation handle. Pass this string verbatim to activate_signal.signal_agent_segment_id — do not pass the signal_id object.

value_type.description: The data type of this signal's values (binary, categorical, numeric)

categories.description: Valid values for categorical signals. Present when value_type is 'categorical'. Buyers must use one of these values in SignalTargeting.values.

range.description: Valid range for numeric signals. Present when value_type is 'numeric'.

signal_type.description: Catalog type of signal (marketplace, custom, owned)

data_provider.description: Human-readable name of the data provider

pricing_options.description: Pricing options available for this signal. The buyer selects one and passes its pricing_option_id in report_usage for billing verification.

errors.description: Task-specific errors and warnings (e.g., signal discovery or pricing issues)

incomplete.description: Declares what the agent could not finish within the caller's time_budget or due to internal limits. Each entry identifies a scope that is missing or partial. Absent when the response is fully complete.

scope.description: 'signals': not all matching signals were returned. 'pricing': signals returned but pricing is absent or unconfirmed. 'catalog': in wholesale mode, whole-catalog enumeration could not complete in the time budget.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what is missing and why.

estimated_wait.description: How much additional time would resolve this scope. Allows the caller to decide whether to retry with a larger time_budget.

catalog_version.description: Opaque token representing the version of the catalog state used to compose this response. Agents that implement conditional-fetch (if_catalog_version) MUST return this on every response so callers can cache and probe later. Callers MUST treat the value as opaque — no format, no ordering, no inspection. Scoped to the request parameters that produced it (discovery_mode, account, filters, destinations, countries). See specs/catalog-change-feed.md.

pricing_version.description: Opaque token representing the version of the pricing layer. When the agent supports independent pricing versioning, pricing_version changes when prices move but catalog_version changes only when structure/metadata moves. Agents not separating these MAY omit pricing_version and use catalog_version for both.

unchanged.description: True ONLY when the request carried if_catalog_version (and/or if_pricing_version) matching the agent's current version, in which case signals[] MUST be omitted; catalog_version (echoed) MUST still be present. Callers receiving unchanged: true MUST NOT mutate their local catalog mirror.

sandbox.description: When true, this response contains simulated data from sandbox mode.

then.description: Catalog-unchanged response: signals MUST be omitted; catalog_version MUST be present.
